title: Razor handle with a pivoting portion
abstract: A handle. The handle includes a main body and a first substantially flat metal arm (24) having a first proximal portion (52) and a first distal end (58), the first proximal portion being rigidly coupled to the main body at a first location, and a second arm (24) having a second proximal portion (52) and a second distal end (58), the second proximal portion being rigidly coupled to the main body at a second location. The first and second distal ends can be in spaced relationship and have pivotally coupled therebetween a pivoting head (22).
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A handle, the handle comprising: â¢ a main body; â¢ a first substantially flat metal arm having a first proximal portion and a first distal end, the first proximal portion being rigidly coupled to the main body at a first location; â¢ a second arm having a second proximal portion and a second distal end, the second proximal portion being rigidly coupled to the main body at a second location; and â¢ the first and second distal ends being in spaced relationship and having pivotally coupled therebetween a pivoting head. 2. The handle of Claim 1, wherein the first distal end and second distal end each comprise a pin member welded thereto. 3. The handle of Claim 1, wherein the first arm comprises a first cylindrical pin member welded at the first distal end and the second arm comprises a second cylindrical pin member welded to the second distal end, and wherein the first pin operatively engages a first receiving opening in the pivoting head and the second pin operatively engages a second receiving opening in the pivoting head. 4. The handle of Claim 1, wherein the first and second arms each comprise metal plates, each metal plate defining a plate plane, and wherein the first arm plate plane is generally co-planar with the second arm plate plane. 5. The handle of Claim 1, wherein the pivoting head comprises a face comprising an elastomeric material. 6. A handle, the handle comprising: â¢ a main body; â¢ a first metal arm having a first proximal portion and a first distal end, the first proximal portion being slidably interlocked at a first location of the main body; â¢ a second metal arm having a second proximal and a second distal end, the second proximal portion being slidably interlocked at a second location of the main; and â¢ the first and second distal ends being in opposed relationship and having pivotally coupled therebetween a pivoting head, the pivoting head having a trapezoidal prism-like shape. 7. The handle of Claim 6, wherein the first arm comprises a first cylindrical pin member welded at the first distal end and the second arm comprises a second cylindrical pin member welded to the second distal end, and wherein the first pin operatively engages a first receiving opening in the pivoting head and the second pin operatively engages a second receiving opening in the pivoting head. 8. The handle of Claim 6, wherein the first and second arms each comprise metal plates, each metal plate defining a plate plane, and wherein the first arm plate plane is generally co-planar with the second arm plate plane. 9. The handle of Claim 6, wherein the pivoting head comprises a face comprising an elastomeric material. 10. A handle, the handle comprising: â¢ a main body; â¢ a first discrete metal arm having a first proximal portion and a first distal end, the first proximal portion being rigidly coupled to the main body at a first protuberance on the main frame; â¢ a second discrete metal arm having a second proximal and a second distal end, the second proximal portion being rigidly coupled to the main body at a second protuberance on the main frame; and â¢ the first and second distal ends being in opposed relationship and having pivotally coupled therebetween a pivoting head, the pivoting head having a trapezoidal prism-like shape. 11. The handle of Claim 13, wherein the handle weighs between 60 grams and 100 grams. 12. The handle of Claim 13, wherein the first distal end and second distal end each comprise a pin member welded thereto. 13. The handle of Claim 13, wherein the first arm comprises a first cylindrical pin member welded at the first distal end and the second arm comprises a second cylindrical pin member welded to the second distal end, and wherein the first pin member and the second pin member reside in a coaxial relationship. 14. The handle of Claim 13, wherein the first and second arms are each rigidly coupled to the main frame by a securement selected from the group consisting of welding, staking, cold heading, adhesive bonding, snap-fit, and frictional engagement. 15. The handle of Claim 13, wherein the first and second arms each comprise metal plates, each metal plate defining a plate plane, and wherein the first arm plate plane is generally co-planar with the second arm plate plane.
